title: Bart Stops To Smell The Roosevelts

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: His signature catchphrase of frustration
jokes.explanation: Homer's iconic catchphrase - perfect character integration and economy, but not original by this point in the series

jokes.text: Beer!
jokes.punchline: His single-word expression of desire
jokes.explanation: Pure Homer character moment - maximum economy but predictable

jokes.text: God, I love to watch that woman get beer.
jokes.setup: Homer watching Marge presumably getting beer
jokes.punchline: His appreciation for the mundane act
jokes.explanation: Homer's simple appreciation for mundane pleasures - solid character work

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.punchline: Second use of his catchphrase
jokes.explanation: Repeat of the same gag - less impact on second use

jokes.text: for things we don't need for kids we didn't want.
jokes.punchline: Admission that the kids were unwanted
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about parenting that's both shocking and efficiently delivered

jokes.text: Yarr! Just plastic. Which is healthier than what you find in the ocean.
jokes.punchline: Plastic being healthier than real ocean pollution
jokes.explanation: Clever environmental commentary disguised as the Sea Captain's observation

jokes.text: I don't want people to know my kids go to this lousy school.
jokes.punchline: Homer's shame about his children's school
jokes.explanation: Homer's honest shame about his situation - good character consistency

jokes.text: I'll just leave the food on your doorstep and ring the bell.
jokes.setup: Auction item is lunch with Principal Skinner
jokes.punchline: Skinner's pre-emptive admission of his social awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment - Skinner's self-awareness of his social ineptitude

jokes.text: I bought one last year and it was crap construction.
jokes.setup: Second grade class bench up for auction
jokes.punchline: CBG's harsh review of children's handicrafts
jokes.explanation: Classic CBG pedantry applied inappropriately to children's work

jokes.text: Paddles down. It's the only way to change the system.
jokes.punchline: Lisa's activist approach to school auction protest
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa moment - applying her political idealism to a trivial situation

jokes.text: I am Edith Knickertwist, an eccentric widow.
jokes.punchline: Bart's over-the-top fake British persona name
jokes.explanation: Absurd character name that's both prim and suggestive - classic Bart mischief

jokes.text: Well, it looks like our model solar-system will finally get an Earth.
jokes.punchline: School's poverty revealed through missing planet
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual joke about school poverty - original concept with perfect understatement

jokes.text: And I've had a proper hardy-har at your expense.
jokes.punchline: Mock-British way of saying he had a laugh
jokes.explanation: Bart's fake British continues - adequate wordplay but not exceptional

jokes.text: Why do I believe everything I hear in a British accent?
jokes.setup: Homer realizing he was fooled by fake British accent
jokes.punchline: His self-aware observation about accent bias
jokes.explanation: Sharp cultural observation delivered efficiently - relatable bias many people have

jokes.text: He has thrust upon us the motley of the tomfool.
jokes.punchline: Overly formal/Shakespearean way to say they were made fools
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chalmers pomposity - overly elaborate language for simple concept

jokes.text: I wore this all night for nothing?
jokes.setup: Auction ending, presumably Frink dressed up for nothing
jokes.punchline: His dismay at wasted formal wear effort
jokes.explanation: Minor character moment - relatable but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Here's $1,000, $2,000, $3,000... Oh, I need more cash. Better go to my invisible ATM.
jokes.setup: Homer pretending to pay Bart's debt
jokes.punchline: Reference to his imaginary money source
jokes.explanation: Homer's creative way to avoid responsibility - good absurdist logic

jokes.text: What? Two dollar charge for non-network bank? - Oh!
jokes.punchline: Even his imaginary ATM has real-world fees
jokes.explanation: Brilliant extension of the invisible ATM gag - even fantasy has modern annoyances

jokes.text: Really? / Nope. Fooled again.
jokes.setup: Chalmers saying he was pleased with Skinner's work
jokes.punchline: Immediate revelation it was sarcasm
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character dynamics but predictable structure

jokes.text: I'd say you're dumb as a post, but at least you can put a sign on a post that says 'fresh strawberries-- one mile.'
jokes.punchline: Even a post has more utility than Skinner
jokes.explanation: Elaborate insult with perfect structure - takes familiar phrase and extends it brilliantly

jokes.text: You are a nitwit in an ill-fitting suit.
jokes.punchline: Specific detail about Skinner's poor tailoring
jokes.explanation: Standard insult elevated by specific visual detail

jokes.text: I'll have you know I'm lop-shouldered.
jokes.punchline: Admitting to physical deformity as defense
jokes.explanation: Classic Skinner pathetic defense - economical and character-appropriate

jokes.text: And I have been since I was hung by my armpits in a North Vietnamese prison.
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to war torture as fashion excuse
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about Skinner's war trauma used as mundane excuse - good character depth

jokes.text: Oh, the list of things you don't know could fill a week's worth of morning announcements, with enough left over for a send-home flyer.
jokes.punchline: School-specific metaphor for vast ignorance
jokes.explanation: Clever school-themed insult with good structure and specificity

jokes.text: There's no need for hyperbole, Seymour.
jokes.punchline: Claiming he wasn't being hyperbolic
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic follow-up - Chalmers' deadpan delivery after elaborate insult

jokes.text: As some guy said to some dude with a beard, 'Death to tyrants.'
jokes.setup: Skinner trying to make historical reference
jokes.punchline: Vague, unhelpful description of famous assassination
jokes.explanation: Perfect Skinner moment - trying to be scholarly but failing at basic details

jokes.text: I'm not the history teacher. Anymore.
jokes.punchline: Admission he's no longer qualified to teach
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Skinner's professional decline revealed through comedy

jokes.text: I guess there's some stones in your leaf bag after all.
jokes.setup: Willie commenting on Skinner standing up to Chalmers
jokes.punchline: Groundskeeper metaphor for having courage
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie line - crude but clever groundskeeper-specific metaphor

jokes.text: I'm gonna call that carpet cleaning service and tell them our hallway doesn't count as a room.
jokes.punchline: His idea of rebellion is a minor service complaint
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy - Skinner's pathetic idea of assertiveness

jokes.text: I know I can superintend-- superintend like the wind-- but teach? It's been years.
jokes.punchline: Awkward adaptation of 'run like the wind' for bureaucracy
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay showing Chalmers' administrative mindset

jokes.text: Wrong room. We're the Fight Club.
jokes.punchline: Students correcting to more violent movie reference
jokes.explanation: Simple pop culture reference correction - efficient but not brilliant

jokes.text: Thank God they never went on to do anything since.
jokes.setup: Simple Minds song from Breakfast Club playing
jokes.punchline: Chalmers' dismissive comment about one-hit wonders
jokes.explanation: Decent cultural observation - economically delivered but not exceptional

jokes.text: Same garbage, different Dumpster.
jokes.setup: Chalmers saying Bart will study with him now
jokes.punchline: Bart's cynical variation on 'same stuff, different day'
jokes.explanation: Great Bart line - perfect economy and attitude, clever phrase variation

jokes.text: Dollar bill guy, five dollar bill guy, sex guy, Will Ferrell, black guy.
jokes.setup: Chalmers asking Bart to identify presidents
jokes.punchline: Bart's crude/ignorant identification of historical figures
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart ignorance - funny misidentification that reveals his shallow pop culture knowledge

jokes.text: I'd act like I'm interested, but inside I'd be bored.
jokes.setup: Chalmers asking if Bart would be interested in cowboy president
jokes.punchline: Bart's honest admission of fake engagement
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on student-teacher dynamics - Bart's brutal honesty

jokes.text: Aha! I'm mad at you for some reason.
jokes.punchline: His confusion about suddenly caring about learning
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bart's self-awareness about his changing attitude

jokes.text: I've never said this before, but I'd like to learn more.
jokes.punchline: Admission that he's never wanted to learn anything before
jokes.explanation: Perfect character development moment - Bart's educational awakening

jokes.text: Man, Teddy Roosevelt killed more Spaniards in one day than most people do in their whole lives.
jokes.setup: Bart learning about Roosevelt's military exploits
jokes.punchline: Treating war deaths like a casual achievement
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about war presented through Bart's innocent enthusiasm

jokes.text: Take that, Standard Oil.
jokes.punchline: His childish battle cry against monopolies
jokes.explanation: Bart applying his aggressive nature to historical economics - good character consistency

jokes.text: Now, to go home and let TV slowly rot it all away.
jokes.punchline: His self-aware prediction about forgetting it all
jokes.explanation: Self-aware media criticism through Bart's honest self-assessment

jokes.text: I thought teachers only went outdoors to smoke and cry.
jokes.punchline: Bart's cynical view of teacher outdoor time
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about teacher burnout delivered through Bart's innocent perspective

jokes.text: Son, have you ever seen a horse your father wasn't betting on?
jokes.punchline: Reference to Homer's gambling problem
jokes.explanation: Good setup/punchline that references Homer's established character flaws

jokes.text: Well, when horses poop, they don't stop. They just keep walking.
jokes.punchline: Bart's crude but accurate animal observation
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart bathroom humor - character-appropriate but not sophisticated

jokes.text: And here I thought God created all this. Thanks for setting me straight.
jokes.setup: Chalmers saying Roosevelt created the national park
jokes.punchline: Bart's sarcastic religious confusion
jokes.explanation: Mild religious humor through Bart's smart-aleck response

jokes.text: You've filled my head with horse poop and atheism. And all these years, I thought I was unteachable.
jokes.setup: Bart reflecting on his learning experience
jokes.punchline: Combining crude horse observation with philosophical confusion
jokes.explanation: Good callback that combines earlier jokes into self-aware character moment

jokes.text: Face on a dime! Face on a mountain!
jokes.setup: Lisa and Bart arguing about Roosevelt accomplishments
jokes.punchline: Competing claims escalating from currency to monument
jokes.explanation: Efficient sibling rivalry moment with good escalation structure

jokes.text: I hated the 'Roosie-velts' and all them family dynasties. The Kennedys, the Bushes, Jon Voigt and Angelina Jolie, Mayor Daley and his smart-ass son, the Daily Show.
jokes.punchline: Mixing legitimate political dynasties with random celebrity families and a TV show
jokes.explanation: Classic Grampa rambling that starts coherent but devolves into nonsense

jokes.text: Well, I like Stephen Colbert, but that's because I don't get the joke.
jokes.punchline: Accidentally explaining why he enjoys satirical comedy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke about how Colbert's satire works on multiple levels

jokes.text: I love Teddy Roosevelt because he had asthma as a boy.
jokes.setup: Kids explaining why they like Roosevelt
jokes.punchline: Milhouse identifying with medical weakness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse moment - identifying with weakness rather than strength

jokes.text: The dude really knew how to rock some jodhpurs.
jokes.punchline: Tough kid admiring historical fashion
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast between Nelson's tough image and fashion appreciation

jokes.text: Does school have to go to summer Jimbo?
jokes.punchline: Jimbo's confused reversal of 'summer school'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jimbo's confusion but trying to participate

jokes.text: You're in my coal mine now, bitches!
jokes.setup: Chalmers called him 'canary in coal mine'
jokes.punchline: Jimbo aggressively claiming ownership of the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Jimbo asserting dominance over Chalmers' metaphor

jokes.text: But I loved reading It's Cool to Cry!
jokes.setup: Chalmers saying it's not cool to cry
jokes.punchline: Milhouse defending emotional children's literature
jokes.explanation: Perfect Milhouse sensitivity - defending emotional expression through literature

jokes.text: This is all well and good, but I must know: what are spectacles?
jokes.setup: Chalmers describing search for Roosevelt's spectacles
jokes.punchline: Tough kid admitting vocabulary ignorance
jokes.explanation: Nelson's tough image contrasted with educational gaps

jokes.text: This place is as beautiful as the side of a Coors beer can. The kind my Dad used to leave in the bathtub.
jokes.punchline: His reference point is beer cans and family dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor about Nelson's troubled home life disguised as nature appreciation

jokes.text: There are no bad fathers in the wilderness, boy.
jokes.punchline: Chalmers' awkward attempt at wilderness wisdom
jokes.explanation: Chalmers trying to be profound but coming off as awkward and insensitive

jokes.text: You know, Bart, morning's when I miss my Rosemary the most. She sure looked good in a pair of jammies.
jokes.setup: Chalmers and Bart having morning conversation
jokes.punchline: Inappropriate oversharing about his deceased wife's appearance
jokes.explanation: Awkward moment of adult oversharing to a child - good cringe comedy

jokes.text: Could this be the moment my life starts to turn around? The moment I...
jokes.punchline: Gets cut off by falling into ravine
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff structure - hope immediately followed by physical comedy

jokes.text: Gravity blows!
jokes.punchline: His angry response to physics
jokes.explanation: Perfect Nelson reaction - treating natural laws as personal enemies

jokes.text: Got me one of them 'abogados' from the bus ads. He said he'll only take 'veinte por ciento,' whatever that is.
jokes.punchline: Not understanding her own lawyer's basic terms
jokes.explanation: Good comedy about predatory lawyers targeting the ignorant

jokes.text: And I am not smiling because I lost the case. Ha-ha! I'm gonna take you for every peso you got.
jokes.punchline: Admitting his bus ad success rate while threatening in Spanish currency
jokes.explanation: Continuation of the predatory lawyer theme with currency confusion

jokes.text: How did she know we get paid in pesos?
jokes.setup: Lawyer threatening to take their pesos
jokes.punchline: Chalmers' confusion about the currency reference
jokes.explanation: Minor reaction joke playing on the peso reference

jokes.text: Maybe it is cool to cry.
jokes.setup: Chalmers losing his job, feeling emotional
jokes.punchline: Reversing his earlier tough stance on crying
jokes.explanation: Good character arc callback - Chalmers learning emotional expression

jokes.text: My book is missing page 32.
jokes.setup: Teacher telling them to look at page 32
jokes.punchline: Bart's book missing the relevant page
jokes.explanation: Standard school dysfunction joke - economical but not particularly clever

jokes.text: My desktop is sticky with soda from the kid that had it before me.
jokes.setup: Teacher telling Bart to put his head on desk
jokes.punchline: Gross condition of school furniture
jokes.explanation: Another school dysfunction joke - slightly gross but predictable

jokes.text: Stupid fish! Don't you want to be my din-ner?!
jokes.punchline: His pathetic attempt to reason with fish
jokes.explanation: Chalmers' desperation manifesting as absurd fish negotiation

jokes.text: I'm not even Kermit Roosevelt.
jokes.setup: Chalmers saying he's no Teddy Roosevelt
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to the even lesser-known Roosevelt
jokes.explanation: Good historical reference showing Chalmers' self-deprecation with obscure Roosevelt

jokes.text: How could we be? We're all in the same place and we don't have computers.
jokes.punchline: Bart's logical breakdown of cyberbullying requirements
jokes.explanation: Smart observation about technology requirements for modern bullying

jokes.text: Marge, I parented!
jokes.setup: Homer asking one question about cyberbullying
jokes.punchline: His pride in minimal parental effort
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - treating basic parenting as major achievement

jokes.text: Oh! Marge, my ice cream's melting and I haven't finished my brownie!
jokes.setup: Bart trying to tell Homer about their plan
jokes.punchline: Homer's food emergency taking priority over parenting
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer priority confusion - food over family

jokes.text: You guys need to get a woman.
jokes.punchline: Bart's mature observation about male behavior
jokes.explanation: Bart showing unexpected maturity about male psychology

jokes.text: There really is a phone in there?
jokes.setup: Bart locking Lisa in closet, she calls mom
jokes.punchline: Lisa's surprise that the closet actually has a phone
jokes.explanation: Minor surprise about school infrastructure

jokes.text: Don't make us start our weekend early.
jokes.punchline: Teacher's sarcastic response to 'threat' of time off
jokes.explanation: Good teacher cynicism about school takeover being a blessing

jokes.text: Suit yourself. We all rubbed it with boogers.
jokes.setup: Skinner threatening to jiggle door handle
jokes.punchline: Bart's gross deterrent strategy
jokes.explanation: Typical gross-out kid humor - character appropriate but juvenile

jokes.text: We've got a 30-year mortgage on that photocopier!
jokes.punchline: School's absurd financing of basic equipment
jokes.explanation: Brilliant commentary on public education funding through absurd mortgage

jokes.text: Ye can't just turn her on and off like that!
jokes.setup: Willie being told to break down door
jokes.punchline: His tractor metaphor with unintended sexual undertone
jokes.explanation: Willie's innocent machinery comment with accidental innuendo

jokes.text: Milhouse, take a nap. You know how cranky you get.
jokes.punchline: Treating Milhouse like a toddler
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bart-Milhouse dynamic - treating friend like difficult child

jokes.text: Are you covering me up?! Yes, I'm afraid we are.
jokes.setup: Homer trying to get TV face time
jokes.punchline: Marge admitting to blocking him from camera
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy about Homer's desperate attention-seeking

jokes.text: It's kind of my way of sending a Christmas card. Happy Holidays, everyone! Family's fine!
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his desire for TV time
jokes.punchline: Using news crisis as holiday greeting
jokes.explanation: Homer's twisted logic about using crisis for social updates

jokes.text: We don't negotiate with terrorists! Or the people that supply our uniforms and weapons. We just pay whatever they say.
jokes.punchline: Admission they negotiate with everyone else
jokes.explanation: Sharp political satire about police budgets and contractor relationships

jokes.text: Well, if a group of Muppets can take Manhattan...
jokes.setup: Discussing whether they can take the building
jokes.punchline: Using Muppet movie as tactical reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Wiggum logic - treating children's entertainment as military strategy

jokes.text: Fine, fine. You're super-duper-intendent.
jokes.punchline: Childish augmentation of superintendent title
jokes.explanation: Simple wordplay showing administrative pettiness

jokes.text: Nothing but hard feelings, Seymour.
jokes.punchline: Chalmers' honest admission of lasting resentment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chalmers moment - direct hostility instead of false pleasantries

jokes.text: Does it look like I took my nap?
jokes.setup: Bart asking if Milhouse took his nap
jokes.punchline: Milhouse's presumably disheveled/cranky appearance
jokes.explanation: Good visual callback to earlier nap joke - Milhouse proving Bart right again

jokes.text: No question: Night at the Museum. One or two? Take your pick, you can't go wrong.
jokes.setup: Bart asking for best Roosevelt book/movie
jokes.punchline: Chalmers recommending shallow Hollywood comedy
jokes.explanation: Chalmers' anti-intellectual recommendation undermines his teaching credibility

tmdb_overview: When superintendent Chalmers is fired for taking Bart and his friends on an unauthorized field trip, the boys hold the school hostage until he is reinstated.
